EcoGrid Advisory helps developers, investors and technical teams progress BESS, renewable generation and large load projects through Australia’s increasingly demanding connection and approval environment.

Discuss your project → View services
NEM Grid connection and regulatory context
BESS System strength, GPS and EMT focus
GW-scale Renewable and large load connection strategy
Technical advisory map
Project type BESS · Solar · Wind · Data centres · Hydrogen
Connection stage Feasibility → GPS → R1/R2 → commissioning
Core risk System strength · Stability · Compliance · Delay
Services

Specialist support where connection risk is highest.

EcoGrid Advisory works at the interface of technical modelling, regulatory obligations and practical project delivery. The focus is clear: identify the real risk, define an evidence-based pathway and avoid late-stage surprises.

01

Grid connection strategy

Structured advice for developers navigating connection applications, access standards, GPS negotiations and NSP/AEMO review processes.

  • Connection risk screening
  • GPS and access standard positioning
  • NSP and AEMO technical engagement
  • Due diligence for acquisition or investment
02

BESS and inverter-based resources

Technical advisory for BESS, wind, solar and hybrid projects where system strength, control interactions and stability performance drive approval risk.

  • System strength and stability review
  • EMT study scope and results assessment
  • GFM and inverter control risk review
  • Model quality and benchmarking support
03

Large load and flexible demand

Support for data centres, hydrogen, electrification and industrial load projects requiring credible assessment of system impact and connection feasibility.

  • Load connection feasibility
  • Power quality and system impact review
  • Network constraint and staging advice
  • Risk mitigation strategy
Capability focus

Technical depth for the parts of the connection process that determine bankability.

The most consequential connection issues rarely sit in a single report. They emerge across models, access standards, network conditions, control behaviour, compliance evidence and stakeholder interpretation.

System strength Assessment framing, risk interpretation and mitigation pathway.
EMT studies Scope review, result interpretation and acceptance criteria.
GPS negotiation Access standard strategy and evidence-based technical positioning.
Power quality Voltage, harmonic and rapid voltage change compliance support.
Model quality Review of assumptions, benchmarking, model limitations and traceability.
Project due diligence Connection risk assessment for developers, investors and acquirers.
Large loads Practical strategy for emerging industrial and flexible demand connections.
Stakeholder engagement Clear technical communication with NSPs, AEMO, OEMs and advisors.
Approach

From uncertainty to a defensible connection pathway.

We combine power system engineering judgement with connection process discipline. The outcome is a clear position on what matters, what evidence is required and what decisions need to be made.

1

Diagnose

Identify the technical and regulatory risks most likely to delay approval or materially affect project value.

2

Evidence

Review models, study assumptions, results and access standard positions against the connection objective.

3

Position

Translate technical findings into a defensible negotiation strategy for NSP, AEMO, OEM and investor stakeholders.

4

Close

Support closure of open technical items with clear actions, acceptance criteria and residual risk ownership.

Why EcoGrid

Principal-level thinking without unnecessary consulting overhead.

EcoGrid Advisory is built for clients who need high-quality, technically credible advice — not generic commentary. We focus on connection risk, model behaviour, regulatory interpretation and practical closure.

Independent · technically rigorous · commercially pragmatic

A

Australian NEM context

Advice grounded in the realities of NSP and AEMO connection assessment, not generic international guidance.

B

Deep power system modelling lens

Strong focus on stability, EMT behaviour, system strength, controls and model evidence.

C

Regulatory and commercial alignment

Technical positions are framed to support approvals, risk allocation and investment decisions.

D

Clear communication

Complex technical issues are translated into clear decisions, actions and stakeholder-ready language.

Have a project facing connection uncertainty?

Contact EcoGrid Advisory to discuss connection strategy, technical due diligence, BESS/grid-forming risks, large load feasibility or GPS negotiation support.
